Trend Scout Daily Market Intelligence — 2026-04-23

Sources: Trendshift.io homepage snapshot for 2026-04-22 rising GitHub engagement; TrustMRR.com verified startup revenue database fetched 2026-04-23 01:00 UTC.

Executive Summary

Today’s hype is overwhelmingly around AI-agent infrastructure, coding-agent skills, MCP/code context, self-hosted intelligence dashboards, and AI-generated media. Today’s revenue leaderboard is less GitHub-native: creator monetization, telehealth/GLP-1, resume/job tools, attribution, AI sales/marketing automation, social scheduling, SEO/AEO, and video/content generation.

Direct name overlap between Trendshift top projects and TrustMRR top revenue startups is zero. Category overlap is strong: AI agents, video generation, social/marketing automation, SEO/AEO, and intelligence/monitoring.

Cross-Reference Analysis

Projects on BOTH Trendshift and TrustMRR

Exact overlap: none found.

The winners are category-level overlaps, not same-name companies:

  1. AI video generation/editing

    • Trendshift: browser-use/video-use, Anil-matcha/Open-Generative-AI
    • TrustMRR: Vid.AI ($94.9k MRR), Speel.co below top 20 ($65.8k MRR)
    • Why it wins: creators and marketers want output, not tooling. Open-source creates experimentation; SaaS packages distribution-ready results.

  2. Agentic social/marketing automation

    • Trendshift: TrendRadar, worldmonitor, agency-agents, agent-skills
    • TrustMRR: Postiz ($97.5k), Cometly ($215.4k), DM Champ ($186.9k), PROSP ($128k), SEOBOT ($74k)
    • Why it wins: agents are valuable when tied to revenue workflows: ads, outreach, SEO, social, conversion.

  3. Coding-agent enablement

    • Trendshift: andrej-karpathy-skills, agent-skills, superpowers, claude-context, opencode
    • TrustMRR: Codédex ($84.3k) is education, not agent infra
    • Why it wins on hype: developers are aggressively retooling their workflows. Revenue capture is still indirect via cloud, enterprise, training, or consulting.

  4. Monitoring/intelligence dashboards

    • Trendshift: worldmonitor, TrendRadar, OpenMetadata
    • TrustMRR: Cometly, SEO STACK, AEO Engine, polysight.app below top 20
    • Why it wins: people pay when dashboards attach to decisions that make/save money.

Trendshift Projects With Zero Clear Monetization

Highest opportunity list:

  • agent-skills / superpowers / andrej-karpathy-skills / taste-skill / awesome-design-md — Package as managed skill libraries, eval suites, team templates, or agent workflow consulting.
  • TrendRadar / worldmonitor — Hosted trend-intelligence SaaS for founders, investors, and comms teams.
  • RAG-Anything / claude-context — Enterprise support, hosted indexing, private-cloud deployment, compliance-ready agent context.
  • video-use / Open-Generative-AI — Hosted video automation with templates, team workflows, and brand safety.
  • Stirling-PDF / Pi-hole — Already utility-heavy; monetization can be managed cloud, business support, or compliance packaging.

TrustMRR Money-Makers Nobody’s Talking About

  • Stan — $3.57M MRR: Creator commerce keeps printing while dev Twitter talks agent frameworks.
  • TrimRx — $307.9k MRR: GLP-1 telehealth remains a cash machine with operational/compliance moat.
  • Kibu — $234.3k MRR: Vertical healthcare SaaS beats generic AI hype by owning compliance workflows.
  • 1Capture — $214.9k MRR: Conversion uplift without product changes is an easy executive buy.
  • Supliful — $197.1k MRR: Creator-led CPG infra monetizes audience-to-product demand.

Conclusions

Top Trends

  1. Agent skills are the new framework layer. Multiple top repos are not apps; they are behavior packs, design files, and methodology for coding agents.
  2. Revenue is strongest where AI touches distribution. Ads, SEO, LinkedIn outreach, social scheduling, video, and conversion tools dominate TrustMRR.
  3. Open-source hype favors infrastructure; MRR favors outcomes. Developers star sandboxes, RAG, context, and agent frameworks; buyers pay for leads, customers, resumes, compliance, and content.
  4. Vertical SaaS is quietly durable. Kibu, TrimRx, medical consulting, and Rezi show specific workflows can outperform broad AI tooling.
  5. Self-hosted remains a strong wedge. Pi-hole, Stirling-PDF, TrendRadar, OpenMetadata: privacy/control attracts attention and can feed hosted/support monetization.

Money vs Hype Gap

  • Hyped but not monetizing: AI skills, CLAUDE.md files, coding-agent taste/design packs, open-source intelligence dashboards.
  • Quietly printing: creator commerce, telehealth, resume SaaS, vertical EHR/compliance, marketing attribution, sales/outreach automation.
  • Best bridge: turn open-source agent excitement into packaged revenue workflows: SEO agents, social agents, codebase context, hosted monitoring, video production.

Opportunities

  • Hosted TrendRadar/worldmonitor for founders/investors: daily competitive intel, Telegram/Slack alerts, saved queries, paid team plans.
  • Agent skill marketplace: curated production-grade skills, evals, versioning, team policy, private libraries.
  • AI coding-context appliance: secure MCP/code search for teams with compliance, audit logs, and IDE/Claude Code integrations.
  • Vertical AI sales agents: DM Champ proves buyers pay for automated closers; clone pattern into dentists, medspas, real estate, legal intake.
  • Self-hosted-to-cloud packaging: Stirling-PDF/Pi-hole-style tools can monetize via managed hosting and business support.

Quick Takes

  • The open-source crowd is building the picks and shovels for agents; the MRR crowd is selling finished outcomes.
  • “Agentic” only monetizes when attached to a painful KPI: leads, conversion, SEO traffic, content throughput, compliance cost.
  • The current gap: tons of starred repos help agents work better, but few give managers a clean ROI dashboard. That’s the product opening.
